result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: F(x) = (n * k * T_0 / x_0) * (x / x_0)^(-2/3) JUSTIFICATION: For a freely jointed chain, the entropy S is a function of the extension x. In an adiabatic process, dS = 0. Since S(x, T) depends on both extension and temperature, the temperature T must change as x changes to keep S constant. For this system, T is proportional to x^(-1/3). The force is derived from the Helmholtz free energy A(x, T), where F = (partial A / partial x)_S. Combining the entropic force component with the adiabatic temperature scaling results in the power law F(x) proportional to x^(-2/3).
